mappings.rationale: Zalasiewicz covers stratigraphic interpretation, the rock record, and the principles of geological dating that are the core methodology of this section.
Thomson covers the nature of fossils, fossilization processes, and the fossil record used for paleontological interpretation and stratigraphic correlation described in this section.
Benton covers the Phanerozoic fossil record, mass extinctions, and the succession of life forms used to interpret the geologic record.
Norman covers dinosaur paleontology and the fossil record of Mesozoic reptiles, directly relevant to the section's treatment of fossil reptiles in the Phanerozoic record.
Wignall covers mass extinctions and background extinctions as revealed in the fossil record, directly corresponding to this section's treatment of species disappearance.
Covers the layered rocks of the Earth's crust, their depositional environments, and the stratigraphic principles used to interpret them.
Tuniz covers radioactive decay and radiometric dating methods, directly relevant to this section's treatment of absolute dating and the radiometric time scale.
Ellam covers isotopic dating techniques and their application to determining the ages of rocks and the Earth, central to this section's absolute dating methods.
Redfern provides context for understanding the geologic record, including Earth's age determination and the major events recorded in rock strata.
The Charlesworths cover evolutionary biology and the concept of fossil succession used for stratigraphic correlation described in this section.
Howard covers Darwin's contributions to understanding fossil succession and the concept of uniformitarianism foundational to interpreting the geologic record.
Molnar discusses seafloor spreading rates determined by absolute dating, and the paleogeographical interpretation of the geologic record.
Bahn covers dating methods applied to archaeological problems, relevant to this section's discussion of relative and absolute dating in archaeological contexts.
Vaughan covers mineral identification relevant to stratigraphic analysis and the mineralogical composition of layered deposits.
Wood covers the fossil record of human ancestors and the dating methods used to place them in geological context, relevant to the Phanerozoic fossil record.
Lenton covers Earth history as recorded in the geological and fossil records, including the co-evolution of life and the planet.
Lowrie covers geophysical methods for interpreting the rock record, including paleomagnetism and the determination of seafloor spreading rates.
